items.title: Short user-facing title
items.description: One sentence that explains the benefit for users.

items.title: Clearer sharing
items.description: Sharing lists is easier to understand and more predictable.

items.title: More reliable list updates
items.description: List changes are safer when connection issues happen.

_instructions: Use app/assets/whats_new/releases.json as the source of truth for the in-app What's New dialog.
Append one release object for the next app version when there are useful user-facing highlights.
Do not backfill old releases unless intentionally curating skipped-version highlights for existing users.
Only userFacing=true items can appear in the dialog.
Use importance values high, medium, or low to prioritize skipped-version summaries.
Use a stable group key to deduplicate related changes across skipped versions.
Exclude dependency updates, logging, analytics, build-system work, refactors, and implementation details.
If a release has no useful user-facing highlights, omit the release entry or leave all items userFacing=false; the app will mark the version seen without showing a dialog.
